title: caltha palustris subsp. radicans
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. radicans, commonly known as marsh marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family. The medicinal properties of Caltha palustris subsp. radicans lie in its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for wound healing. Caltha palustris subsp. radicans is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its root and leaves can also be used in salves and ointments for topical application.
